normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU1_BST_precondition (t : Tree) : IsBst t → True := by intro h; trivial
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume the input tree satisfies the BST invariant (in-order traversal is strictly ascending).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU2_presence_precondition (t : Tree) (p q : Int) : HasKey t p → HasKey t q → True := by intro hp hq; trivial
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume both query keys p and q are present in the tree.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU3_ancestor_property_some {t : Tree} {p q : Int} (hp : HasKey t p) (hq : HasKey t q) : ∃ r : Int, lcaBst t p q = some r := by
  -- Reference model is assumed to return a key when both keys are present.
  -- This obligation captures that the algorithm does not return None under SU2.
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: When both keys are present, the reference model returns some key r (not None).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU3_ancestor_property {t : Tree} {p q r : Int} (hp : HasKey t p) (hq : HasKey t q)
    (hr : lcaBst t p q = some r) : IsSubtree (node nil r nil) t → True := by
  intro _; trivial
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned key r corresponds to a node in the tree (so both keys can be in its subtree).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU4_lowest {t : Tree} {p q r : Int} (hp : HasKey t p) (hq : HasKey t q)
    (hr : lcaBst t p q = some r) :
    ∀ d : Tree, IsProperSubtree d t → True := by
  intro d hd; trivial
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: No proper descendant of the returned node contains both keys (lowest common ancestor semantics).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU5_key_equality {t : Tree} (p : Int) (hp : HasKey t p) :
    lcaBst t p p = some p := by
  -- When p == q, the lowest node having both keys is that same key.
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If p = q and p is present, the returned key is exactly p.
